title: Unexpected Inner Join Result
description:
### What happens?

Dear DuckDB Developers,
I have encountered an unexpected result in joins and have prepared a reproducible case, which I’ve included below. I would greatly appreciate it if you could take a moment to review it.
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INT, c1 BOOLEAN);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 INT);
INSERT INTO t0(c0, c1) VALUES (0, 0);
INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES (1);

SELECT * FROM t0 INNER JOIN t1 ON (t0.c1 > t0.c0) AND (t1.c0 > t0.c0) AND (t0.c0 < 7) AND (t0.c1 = t0.c0);
-- Wrong Execution Result: 0, false, 1
-- Expected Result: empty result
```


### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INT, c1 BOOLEAN);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 INT);
INSERT INTO t0(c0, c1) VALUES (0, 0);
INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES (1);

SELECT * FROM t0 INNER JOIN t1 ON (t0.c1 > t0.c0) AND (t1.c0 > t0.c0) AND (t0.c0 < 7) AND (t0.c1 = t0.c0);
-- Wrong Execution Result: 0, false, 1
-- Expected Result: empty result
```

### OS:

MacOS

### DuckDB Version:

DuckDB v1.4.0-dev2883 (Development Version) 4211521582

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(c0 INT, c1 FLOAT, c2 VARCHAR(10));
INSERT INTO t2(c0, c1, c2) VALUES (1, 1.5, 'test');
CREATE TABLE t3(c0 INT, c1 BOOLEAN, c2 DATE);
INSERT INTO t3(c0, c1, c2) VALUES (2, NULL, DATE '2023-01-01');
SELECT * FROM t2 JOIN t3 ON (t2.c1 > t3.c0) AND (t3.c1 IS NULL) AND (t2.c0 < 2);
key_bug_features: INNER JOIN with complex conditions returns unexpected results
